summary: mddriver fetches and executes remote code from anonymous paste host on require()
details: Package is advertised as a MongoDB connection driver, but index.js is a copy of Node's built-in path module with a dropper appended. On require(), an immediately-invoked function decodes a base64 string to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/C4H0M (an anonymous, mutable JSON paste host), fetches its `content` field, and executes it via a dynamically built Function constructor. The destination URL is hidden behind atob() and the executor is constructed indirectly to evade casual review. Any process that imports mddriver will run whatever JavaScript the paste currently serves, with full Node privileges of the importing process. The deceptive package name/description is a cover for the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
